your_book:
Wednesday was a broadly constructive session for Jaleel's book, though the headline numbers mask a real split. Adobe led the day's winners, adding 1.71% (+$59) to bring its value to $3,518 — welcome given the stock is still sitting on an -8.4% open loss and carrying an AI-disruption risk flag. Equifax popped 2.80% despite being a small position ($172), and SPGI, GILD, and AVGO each ticked up around 1.3%. The defensive anchor Abbott ($2,589) crept up 0.35% ahead of its next catalyst on July 16 — tomorrow — where the EPS guide trim will be in focus. Danaher and BSX also added modest green. The book closed at $21,240 with cash near zero ($75), so there is no dry powder to act on opportunities.

The drag came from two names. Rocket Lab dropped another 3.31% (-$68) to $1,981, deepening an already punishing -18.1% open loss on a position rated AVOID with a QARP score of just 40.9 — the system is flagging exit while the position keeps bleeding. ServiceNow slipped -0.11%, barely notable on its own, but a Steve Eisman 'AI has no moats' headline landed squarely on its thesis today, the day before its July 22 earnings catalyst. That's noise worth watching, not panic — NOW's fundamentals (Q1 strong, IBM AI deal) remain intact — but it is the book's third-largest position at $2,409 and carries a high-multiple vulnerability if software sentiment cracks.

The drift story is concentrated in three names: ADBE (-8.4% open loss, Strong Buy), BSX (-10.1%, Strong Buy, guidance slashed), and RKLB (-18.1%, AVOID). In ADBE and BSX, thesis and price are diverging in opposite directions — the system still likes the quality but the market is disagreeing. In RKLB, the system agrees with the market and has said AVOID; the position is simply not being acted on. With unrealized losses at -$450.50 (-2.08%) and zero cash buffer, the RKLB drag is a real cost of inaction.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated, -18.1% open loss, still held at 9.3% weight
attention.note: The system rates this AVOID (QARP 40.9) and the position has lost over 18% — with no cash buffer to redeploy, continuing to hold is an active choice that needs a concrete rationale or an exit plan.

attention.headline: EPS guide-cut catalyst hits tomorrow, July 16
attention.note: Abbott's FY EPS outlook was already trimmed on deal financing and a weak flu season; tomorrow's report is the next live test of whether that flag escalates from yellow to red.

attention.headline: Earnings July 22 with 'AI no moats' narrative emerging day before
attention.note: ServiceNow is the book's third-largest position at a high multiple, and a prominent short-seller's AI-moat skepticism surfaced today — exactly the sentiment that leads a software de-rate if the July 22 print disappoints.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, mgmt guides flat through Q3, next print July 29
attention.note: FY26 growth was cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% and the position is already -10.1% open; the July 29 earnings are the next checkpoint on whether the slowdown is stabilizing or worsening.
